decision: Adopted the starter-pack anti-drift doc system
why: Docs split by what drifts (intent/laws/decisions/past/future/design/evidence); the immutable layer is enforced below the agent so a fresh session cannot silently re-decide settled questions
rejected: relying on a vision doc alone (grounds intent, not decisions)
instructions without enforcement

decision: Concept is Aegis — a procurement TRUST loop (stockout-rescue with fraud defense in the loop)
why: Fuses the two cash stories into one; answers the unspoken objection to every autonomous-spend demo ('what stops it getting scammed?'); the self-correction loop (source->verify->fail->blacklist->retry) is literally the judged theme on screen
rejected: Concept A pure stockout-rescue (no answer to fraud)
Concept B customer-checkout fraud (reads as Stripe Radar; data absent in Zero)
Concept C cargo rerouter (worst data availability, least demoable)
pure-software agent-security/prompt-injection ideas (team wanted real-world P&L)

decision: Paid verification runs only on Zero.xyz tools that provably exist and settle; no mocked verification vendor
why: Deep research (3-0, two independent fetches of zero.xyz/browse + zero-plugins repo) proved Zero does not broker credit-bureau/registry/fraud-score/freight data but does broker enrichment (Apollo/PDL/Crustdata), scraping (Firecrawl/BrightData), news (serp), and comms (StableEmail/StablePhone). Assemble the fraud verdict from enrichment + domain-age scrape + adverse-media + AI call; engineer the planted fraud vendor to fail all four
rejected: buying a verified-supplier-registry / credit-bureau / fraud-score via Zero (does not exist)
standing up a fake registry we call through Zero (fakes the exact moment Zero judges scrutinize; guts the honest-trust-layer pitch)

decision: Dropped Fillmore; PO email sent via StableEmail
why: Deep research confirmed (3-0) Fillmore is recruiting-only (sourcing/outreach/scheduling in Slack) and cannot draft purchase orders; using it for procurement is a domain mismatch a Metaview judge won't reward
rejected: using Fillmore to draft/send the procurement PO

decision: Tool roles fixed to genuine fits; Nexla via FlexFlow (GA) not MCP Studio; Akash is coverage not a prize play
why: Zero=wallet+paid verification (prize), Pomerium=identity-aware gate on the payment/PO API attested-only (prize), Nexla=real-time trigger via FlexFlow GA (MCP Studio is Early Access, same-day risk), Akash=hosting only (weak prize case, don't over-invest)
rejected: Nexla MCP Studio on the day (EA risk)
forcing an Akash GPU/compute angle we don't need

decision: The product name is StockShield and the product remains vendor-impersonation defense for stockout procurement
why: The user selected StockShield; technical review found that merging customer chargeback screening with supplier verification creates two products, two data models, and a weaker three-minute story
rejected: Aegis as the public name
buyer identity audits
bot-order purging
claiming that customer demand validation and supplier verification are one loop

decision: Pomerium authorizes vendor-scoped machine identities; it does not read an arbitrary SQLite attestation or trust a vendorId request body
why: The agent is the network caller, while the vendor is an object in the request. A shared agent identity cannot distinguish vendors. The verifier must release a vendor-scoped capability, Pomerium must validate that identity, and the origin must bind Pomerium's signed subject to the vendor path and PO constraints
rejected: Pomerium reads local SQLite directly
checking only X-Attestation header presence
one shared agent service token for every vendor
calling an Express 403 a Pomerium denial

decision: Verification is a deterministic vendor-risk policy with eligible, ineligible, and insufficient-evidence outcomes
why: Domain age, missing news, and LLM prose are weak fraud evidence. The policy uses independent evidence classes, hard failures for identity/payee mismatch or typosquatting, versioned evidence hashes, expiry, amount and quote binding; the LLM may explain but may not adjudicate
rejected: calling every rejected fixture fraudulent
using an LLM verdict as independent evidence
claiming credit bureau, bank verification, or business registry data without a live provider

decision: The autonomous 403 occurs before verification, not as a forced purchase after blacklisting
why: Forcing the agent to retry a candidate it already classified ineligible looks scripted and irrational. The cheaper candidate is attempted with the authenticated general-agent identity, the live policy denial teaches the environmental constraint, and the agent replans to acquire evidence without human intervention
rejected: manual force-fraud button
post-blacklist policy probe presented as normal agent behavior
claiming an origin 403 proves Pomerium

decision: Local, live-evidence, and prize modes have separate readiness proofs
why: Fixture data, an origin guard, and environment labels can validate development without proving sponsor integration. Each live claim now requires an external artifact and npm run doctor:prize fails closed until the required configuration and service lock exist
rejected: silent fixture fallback
source labels as sponsor proof
recording before live readiness passes

decision: The demo is critical-spares-first and starts from an always-on threshold monitor
why: A direct run button undercuts the autonomy criterion. A deterministic node failure consumes a spare, while the independent monitor emits the same versioned event shape as Nexla and starts the loop without another operator action
rejected: presenting a local source label as Nexla proof
manual run as the primary stage path
claiming an illustrative downtime rate is measured

decision: The approved demand-audit and protected-purge branch is deferred until the core sponsor proof is green
why: The branch adds buyer evidence, a second deterministic policy, a purge capability, a second protected mutation, queue state, and a second dashboard workflow. Adding it before live Zero and Pomerium evidence would reduce technical completeness and demo reliability
rejected: claiming fixture buyer evidence is paid Zero evidence
shipping an unprotected purge action
letting the extension block the tested procurement slice
